food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken, burgers, sides, and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Jollibee-specific combo meals. FOODON_00002490 (fast food meal) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (fast food composite meal) is the closest parent. No Jollibee-specific or Philippine fast food combo code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a branded fast food combo meal at this specificity level.

serving.description: 1 full Value for Two Meal C (Dinner) — entire meal for two persons
serving.natural_language_portion: One complete Value for Two Meal C (Dinner) set comprising 4 pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken, 2 Cheesy Yumburgers, 2 side dishes, and 2 drinks, estimated at approximately 1,400 g total for two persons sharing.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation from fried chicken (SR Legacy 05209), beef burger with cheese (SR Legacy 21110), white rice (SR Legacy 20045), and carbonated soft drink (SR Legacy 14400); cross-referenced with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information for Chickenjoy and Cheesy Yumburger
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Jollibee Foods Corporation published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact combo meal. Per-100g values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: 4 pieces Chickenjoy (~560 g total, ~280 kcal/100g, ~18g protein, ~17g fat, ~10g carb per 100g based on USDA SR Legacy fried chicken skin-on bone-in 05209); 2 Cheesy Yumburgers (~280 g total, ~270 kcal/100g, ~14g protein, ~13g fat, ~25g carb per 100g based on USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger 21110); 2 sides assumed as white rice (~200 g total, ~130 kcal/100g per USDA SR Legacy 20045); 2 drinks assumed as regular cola (~360 g total, ~42 kcal/100g per USDA SR Legacy 14400). Total estimated meal weight ~1,400 g. Weighted average per 100g computed across all components. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of mixed fast food meals — analogous estimate based on GI values for fried chicken (GI ~40), white rice (GI ~72, Atkinson et al. 2008), cola beverages (GI ~63), and cheeseburger bun (GI ~70); weighted composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Jollibee combo meal. GI estimated as weighted average of component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008: fried chicken skin-on (~40, low GI protein-fat dominant component), white rice (~72, Jasmine-type), cola drink (~63), burger bun wheat bread (~70). Component weights: chicken 40%, burger 20%, rice 14%, drink 26%. Weighted GI ≈ 56 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (56 × 260) / 100 = 145.6, rounded to 145.0. Carbohydrate per serving estimated at ~260 g total for the full 1,400 g meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg/100g; estimated ~4,340 mg per full meal for two)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to its very high sodium load from fried chicken seasoning, processed cheese, and burger sauces, which can significantly elevate blood pressure; if consumed, portion should be limited to one piece of chicken and half a burger with no added sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~145 per full meal), refined carbohydrates from buns, rice, and sugary drinks
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal in full-serving quantities due to its high glycemic load driven by white rice, burger buns, and cola beverages; if consumed, substituting the sugary drink with water and replacing rice with a non-starchy vegetable side can substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), cholesterol (~48 mg/100g), and high sodium from deep-fried chicken and processed cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of deep-fried chicken, processed cheese, and refined carbohydrates contributes to elevated LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and blood pressure; the full meal for two exceeds recommended daily saturated fat and sodium limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Value for Two Meal C (Dinner) is a fast food combo meal sold at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, designed to serve two people. It consists of four pieces of Chickenjoy — Jollibee's signature bone-in, skin-on deep-fried chicken marinated in a proprietary blend of spices and coated in a crispy, seasoned batter — two Cheesy Yumburgers (beef patties topped with processed cheese on a soft white bun), two side dishes (typically white steamed rice or french fries), and two drinks (typically regular cola or juice). Chickenjoy is the flagship product of Jollibee Foods Corporation, a Philippine-origin fast food chain with a strong presence across Southeast Asia and the Filipino diaspora globally. The meal is characterised by its savoury, mildly sweet fried chicken flavour profile, the soft and slightly sweet burger bun, and the creamy processed cheese. Nutritionally, the full meal is energy-dense (~2,770 kcal total), high in sodium (~4,340 mg), saturated fat, and refined carbohydrates, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is certified Halal at Jollibee Singapore outlets. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component. Per 100g, the meal provides approximately 198 kcal, 11.2 g protein, 9.4 g fat, and 18.6 g carbohydrate.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact Jollibee combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA SR Legacy data for fried chicken, cheeseburger, white rice, and cola. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: This record covers the full Value for Two Meal C (Dinner) as sold at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore via field survey. The meal is intended for two persons sharing. Per-100g values are estimated from component-weighted averaging; total meal weight estimated at ~1,400 g. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. The (D) suffix in the original field survey listing denotes Dinner variant. Sides composition assumed as white rice based on typical Jollibee Singapore menu; actual sides may vary (rice or fries). Drinks assumed as regular cola; actual drink choice may vary. Glycemic load per serving is calculated for the full 1,400 g meal for two persons (~260 g total carbohydrate). If consumed per person (half the meal), glycemic load per person is approximately 72.5. Halal certification applies to Jollibee Singapore outlets. Price SGD 28.60 as listed on field survey data at time of data collection.
